title: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.title: How to Make Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce — Easy Mexican Recipe
seo.metaDescription: Learn how to make authentic Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce. This easy Mexican recipe includes step-by-step instructions, tips, and serving ideas.

longTailKeywords: how to make Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce

seo.imageAlt: Authentic Mexican Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce recipe
seo.imageCaption: Delicious homemade Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce — a beloved Mexican classic

description: Pollo en Mole is a rich and complex Mexican dish featuring tender chicken simmered in a deeply flavorful mole sauce. This traditional recipe combines a symphony of chiles, spices, nuts, and a touch of chocolate, creating an unforgettable culinary experience that embodies the heart of Mexican cuisine. Prepare to delight your senses with this authentic and beloved Mexican classic.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Place the chicken pieces in a large pot with 4 cups of chicken broth, half an onion, and 2 garlic cloves. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and simmer until the chicken is cooked through and tender, about 25-30 minutes. Remove chicken and set aside. Strain the broth and reserve it for the mole sauce.

instructions.title: Prepare the Chiles
instructions.text: Heat a dry comal or skillet over medium heat. Toast the dried ancho, mulato, and pasilla chiles for 1-2 minutes per side until fragrant, being careful not to burn them. Transfer the toasted chiles to a bowl, cover with hot water, and let them rehydrate for about 15-20 minutes until soft. Drain and set aside.

instructions.title: Toast Nuts, Seeds, and Tortillas
instructions.text: In the same dry skillet, toast the almonds, peanuts, and sesame seeds separately until lightly golden and fragrant. Remove and set aside. Next, lightly toast or fry the corn tortillas until slightly crisp. Break them into pieces. This adds thickness and flavor to the mole.

instructions.title: Sauté Aromatics and Spices
instructions.text: Heat 2 tablespoons of vegetable oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add the chopped tomatoes, tomatillos, remaining onion, and garlic. Sauté until softened, about 5-7 minutes. Add the ground cumin, cinnamon, cloves, and dried oregano, stirring for 1 minute until fragrant.

instructions.title: Blend the Mole Sauce
instructions.text: Combine the rehydrated chiles, sautéed aromatics and spices, toasted nuts, sesame seeds, and toasted tortilla pieces in a blender. Add about 2 cups of the reserved chicken broth. Blend until completely smooth. You may need to do this in batches. If the mixture is too thick, add more broth gradually until it reaches a thick, pourable consistency.

instructions.title: Simmer the Mole
instructions.text: Heat the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in the same pot. Carefully pour the blended mole sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into the hot oil, pressing down on the solids to extract all liquid. This step is crucial for a smooth mole. Bring the sauce to a gentle simmer, stirring constantly to prevent sticking. Add the Mexican chocolate and sugar or piloncillo. Continue to simmer for at least 20-25 minutes, stirring frequently, until the sauce thickens and deepens in color. Season with salt to taste. It should taste rich and complex.

instructions.title: Combine and Serve
instructions.text: Add the cooked chicken pieces to the mole sauce. Simmer for another 10-15 minutes, allowing the chicken to absorb the flavors of the mole. Serve hot, garnished with a sprinkle of sesame seeds. Pollo en Mole is traditionally served with warm corn tortillas and Mexican rice.

tags: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce
tips: For a truly authentic mole, ensure your chiles are properly toasted but not burnt before rehydrating. This brings out their deep, smoky flavors without bitterness.
The secret to a smooth mole is blending it thoroughly and then passing it through a fine-mesh sieve. This removes any fibrous bits from the chiles and nuts, resulting in a silky texture.
Mole sauce tastes even better the next day as the flavors meld. Store leftover mole in an airtight container in the refrigerator for up to 3-4 days, or freeze for longer storage.
article: Pollo en Mole Chicken Mole Sauce is a beloved Mexican Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Mexican. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
